title: salix repens subsp. angustifolia
intro: Salix repens subsp. angustifolia, also known as creeping willow, is a subspecies of the willow plant that belongs to the Salicaceae family. It is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Western Asia, with narrow leaves and small, catkin-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to its salicylic acid content, a compound similar to aspirin. Herbal preparations of creeping willow, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and may also provide relief from stress and anxiety due to its adaptogenic properties.
